Choice of Law — General Rule.rule: A court applies its own procedural law but applies substantive law according to choice of law rules.
Choice of Law — General Rule.application: Because the issue is procedural, forum law applies.

Traditional Approach — Vested Rights.rule: Under the traditional approach, the law of the place where the rights vested governs.
Traditional Approach — Vested Rights.application: Because the rights vested in another state, that law applies.

Modern Approach — Most Significant Relat.rule: Under the modern approach, the law of the state with the most significant relationship to the issue applies.
Modern Approach — Most Significant Relat.application: Because the forum has the most significant relationship, its law applies.

Restatement (Second) Factors.rule: The most significant relationship test considers contacts and policy interests of the states involved.
Restatement (Second) Factors.application: Because the forum has stronger policy interests, its law governs.

Torts — Choice of Law.rule: In tort cases, courts apply the law of the place of the injury under the traditional approach.
Torts — Choice of Law.application: Because the injury occurred in another state, that law applies.

Contracts — Choice of Law.rule: In contract cases, courts apply the law of the place of contracting or the law chosen by the parties.
Contracts — Choice of Law.application: Because the contract was formed elsewhere, that law applies.

Contractual Choice of Law Clause.rule: A contractual choice of law clause is enforced unless unreasonable or contrary to public policy.
Contractual Choice of Law Clause.application: Because the clause is reasonable, it is enforced.

Property — Real Property.rule: The law of the situs governs issues involving real property.
Property — Real Property.application: Because the land is located in another state, that law governs.

Property — Personal Property.rule: The law of the owner’s domicile governs issues involving personal property.
Property — Personal Property.application: Because the owner is domiciled elsewhere, that law applies.

Family Law — Marriage.rule: A marriage valid where celebrated is generally valid everywhere.
Family Law — Marriage.application: Because the marriage was valid where formed, it is recognized.

Family Law — Divorce.rule: A state may grant divorce if one spouse is domiciled in the state.
Family Law — Divorce.application: Because one spouse was domiciled in the forum, the divorce is valid.

Full Faith and Credit — Judgments.rule: A final judgment rendered with jurisdiction must be recognized by other states.
Full Faith and Credit — Judgments.application: Because jurisdiction existed, the judgment must be enforced.

Penal Laws Exception.rule: A forum will not enforce the penal laws of another state.
Penal Laws Exception.application: Because the law is penal, it is not enforced.

Procedural vs Substantive Law.rule: Procedural matters are governed by forum law, while substantive matters are governed by choice of law principles.
Procedural vs Substantive Law.application: Because the rule affects procedure, forum law applies.

Public Policy Exception.rule: A forum may refuse to apply foreign law that violates a strong public policy.
Public Policy Exception.application: Because the law violates public policy, it is not applied.

Statute of Limitations.rule: Statutes of limitations are generally considered procedural and governed by forum law.
Statute of Limitations.application: Because limitations are procedural, forum law applies.
